title: Automated source/grant-change watcher (hash-diff vs. snapshots)
context: Object records store a snapshot (committed copy + SHA-256 hash + web-archive URL) of the governing license or grant at the time of ingest. If the upstream source or grant later changes — a license is amended, a CC collection changes terms, or a partner grant expires — the project must detect the drift before any further work or delivery proceeds on the affected object. M0 relied on a minimal/manual check; M1 requires this to be automated and running on a schedule or in CI.
objective: Build a source/grant-change watcher script that: for each object record, re-fetches the allow-listed source URL or grant document; recomputes the SHA-256 hash; compares it against the stored snapshotHash in the object record; and emits a drift-detected warning for any mismatch, flagging the affected object(s) for re-verification before further work or delivery. Wire the script to run on a schedule (e.g., via CI cron or a scheduled workflow) and ensure flagged objects cannot proceed to new description or delivery tasks until re-verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Script re-fetches each allow-listed source URL or grant document referenced in object records, recomputes the SHA-256 hash, and compares against the stored snapshotHash
Emits a clear drift-detected warning (logged and optionally as a CI failure or GitHub issue) for any object whose current upstream hash does not match the stored snapshot
Flagged objects are clearly identified so re-verification can be completed before any further description, translation, or delivery work proceeds
Script runs on a defined schedule (CI cron or equivalent) as well as on demand; schedule and re-verification gate documented
Script follows the agent-neutral core pattern: no vendor-specific logic; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; a changeset is added and commits are signed off (DCO)
resources: C:/code/elyos/CLAUDE.md (architecture: no vendor logic in core)
output: PR adding source/grant-change watcher script and CI schedule configuration; drift-detected objects flagged for re-verification
